titleStmt.title: SII 19.342: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao, Dines Chandra Sircar and G.S. Gai
No. 342. (A.R. No. 194 of 1907.) TIRUVIḌAIMARUDŪR, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT. ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, MAHĀLIṄGASVĀMI TEMPLE.

summary.p: The details of the date correspond to A.D. 984, October 6 in the reign of Uttama-Chōḷa. The inscription is damaged. It seems to register a sale of land belonging to the temple of Tiruviḍaimaruduḍaiyār by the assembly of Tiraimūr, the dēvakanmi of the temple, the nagarattār of Tiruviḍaimarudil and Iṅgaṇāṭṭup-Pallavarayar the trustee of the temple, to a certain Echchilkaṇḍa-Rāvaṇaṉ of Dēvaṅguḍi in Miraikūṟṟam on the northern bank of the river, for the purpose of raising money for some gold ornaments to the god.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī . ko
pparakecaripa
nmaṟki yāṇṭu
10 4- Āva
tu tiruviṭai
marutuṭaiyār
tevakanmikaḷ tirai
mur sabhaiyārum ti
ruviṭaimarutil nakara
ttārum Uṭaiyār śrīkā
rya mārāykiṉṟa Iṅkaṇāṭṭup
pallavaraiyaru muṭaiyār ko
yiliṉ teṟkil tirukkok
kārāṇikke poṉa tiruvācaliṉ
kiḻparaṭai Iluppaikkiḻe
tulānāyaṟṟut tiṅkaḷ kiḻa
maipeṟṟa cataiyattiṉāṉ
ṟu Iruntu vaṭakarai miṟaikkū
ṟṟattu tevaṅkuṭit tevaṅ
kuṭaiyāṉ Eccilkaṇṭa rāva
ṇaṉukku viṟṟukkuṭutta nila
māvatu tiruviṭaimarutuṭai
yār koyiliṉ vaṭakkil ma kkalāṉṟu kai
yāṟṟiṉ kiḻaicceykku Ellai piṇam pokum va
ḻikku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai tirukkuḷaṅkarai
kku vaṭakkum melpārkkellaiy mayi
lukku kiḻakkum vaṭapārkkellai paḻaṅkāvirikkut te
ṟkum Ivvicaitta perunāṉkellaiyilum naṭuvupaṭṭa nilam mikutikku
ṟaimai Uḷḷaṭaṅka puṉceyyum kollaiyum nirnilamum koṇṭu ka
lli Ivaṉe tiru lattil vāṉavaṉ peyar ceyta taṉmattukku
niṅku nilam A raikkāṇi nikki niṉṟa nilam koṇṭu Uṭaiya Irupatiṉ
poṉṉiṉ kai ceyya cāmarai Oṉṟukku poṉ 2 10 5- m Āka
tta ce ṉukku
Digital edition of SII 19.342 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
